review_text: I am very disappointed with my recent experience with Autodoc.I placed an order for Pirelli tires on 27.02.2026 and received them on 11.03.2026. However, I later discovered that the tires have DOT codes from 01/2023 and 02/2023. This means the tires were already over 3 years old at the time of delivery, which is not what I expect when purchasing a “new” product in 2026.Since then, I have been dealing with this issue for almost a month. During this time, I have not been able to complete a necessary tire change, which has affected my ability to use my vehicle safely.In addition, I have had to store, move, and handle four tires multiple times due to this situation, which caused unnecessary physical effort and inconvenience — all because of incorrect or misleading expectations regarding the product.The most frustrating part is the lack of a satisfactory solution. I requested a reasonable compensation, considering the age of the tires and the inconvenience caused. Instead, I received only a minimal offer and repeated delays.I am based in Switzerland, and returning the tires is not simple or cost-effective. Despite this, I was informed that I may need to handle the return logistics myself, which I find unacceptable given that the issue is not caused by me.For a company of this size, I expected a much more customer-oriented approach and a fair resolution.If this matter is not resolved properly, I will consider taking further steps through consumer protection channels.I sincerely hope Autodoc will take this complaint seriously and provide a fair and reasonable solution.
supplier_response: Hi Furkan! We are sorry to know that you left unsatisfied with your purchase. Please write to us at trust@autodoc.de so that we can discuss options for resolving this situation. We hope for a positive outcome and thank you for your purchase. Sincerely, AUTODOC team

review_text: I ordered a SACHS flywheel (2294 501 267) from Autodoc, paying a premium SACHS price.What I received was a broken, recycled LUK flywheel, inside a SACHS box, tied together with zip ties and taped shut. The flywheel had huge play throughout, making it completely unsafe for installation.SACHS/ZF confirmed that this flywheel is not even produced by SACHS, but sourced from LUK for rare VWAG models. Yet Autodoc never disclosed this anywhere, selling it as SACHS at a premium price.The price difference is shocking: in Bulgaria it can be €350–€450, while on other European sites it is only about €150, yet the product inside is identical to a much cheaper LUK part. Autodoc profits massively by misleading customers.This is misleading, unsafe, and completely unacceptable. I returned the part the same day, but Autodoc failed to resolve the issue, leaving my car unusable for 2 months and causing 4 months of delays, frustration, and extra costs.Autodoc must stop misleading customers and clearly disclose when parts are sourced from third parties, especially when they are broken or unsafe.
supplier_response: Hello Kristian Karakashev! We sincerely apologize for the experience you’ve had with the flywheel. We understand your frustration regarding the incorrect part, safety concerns, and the inconvenience caused by the delays. Please be informed that our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to update with your case in detail and assist you further to resolve this matter. Your feedback will also be used to improve our product verification and disclosure processes. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Very disappointing and misleading experience with AutodocMy experience with Autodoc has been extremely frustrating and disappointing.I ordered spare parts after Autodoc’s website explicitly confirmed compatibility with my vehicle. My VIN number is already saved in my personal account, and based on this information, the website assured that the parts were suitable. However, when the parts arrived, they turned out to be completely incompatible.As a result, I had to return the items and paid shipping costs twice (to and from Geneva), totaling approximately CHF 85. Autodoc refunded only the price of the parts and refused to reimburse the shipping costs, even though the mistake was entirely theirs.Instead of taking responsibility, they attempted to blame the customer by claiming that selecting parts by make and model is “not accurate.” This raises a serious question: why does their website confirm compatibility if it is unreliable?This was not my first bad experience with Autodoc. Previously, I was offered a compensation voucher for a similar mistake, which later turned out to be invalid and unusable. Now they repeat the same approach by offering a small discount coupon instead of refunding the actual costs caused by their error.In summary:Incorrect and incompatible partsCustomer forced to pay for the company’s mistakesUnreliable vouchersAvoidance instead of real solutionsA very poor customer experience. I will not use Autodoc again and cannot recommend them.
supplier_response: Hello Sabah Jassem! We sincerely apologize for the inconvenience and frustration you’ve experienced. Please be aware that the parts displayed on our website whether selected through the vehicle selector or by entering your registration number are intended as general recommendations. Because vehicles often have unique specifications beyond just the model and body type, the most accurate way to confirm compatibility is by using the Vehicle Identification Number (VIN) or the Original Equipment Number (OEN). Even if your VIN is stored in your account, our system does not automatically verify compatibility against it. This important step must be carried out by our trained specialists to ensure the correct fit. Our customer support team is always available to help with a full compatibility check, as product listings only provide technical details and cannot guarantee suitability for every vehicle. Please rest assured that our Quality Control Manager will be reaching out to you shortly to personally review your case and provide further assistance, ensuring the matter is handled properly. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: I ordered a steering rack for my 2009 Audi A4 B8 2.0l TDI (143hp) 2 weeks ago. The delivery was fast, it arrived in only a few days, which i liked. Yet it turns out a part was missing in the delivery: The metal plug. Instead, it was delivered with a plastic one to keep the dust from entering. According to my local mechanic, without the right plug / lock, the plastic one wouldn't keep up with the oil pressure and it would just explode.I have tried to contact AUTODOC in multiple ways.-The AI assistant on their app told me I'd get an e-mail in 24-48 hours, 4 days later I haven't received anything.-I have called their numbers in hope of reaching an actual human instead of an AI, but guess what. Dan the virtual assistant picked up and he was no help at all. I could repeat a sentence 3 times and he wouldn't understand a thing I said, so after some time he gave up and said "We're happy we could solve your problem!"Next I wanted to see if it's possible to atleast return the steering rack, but no, "Unfortunately, this product is not eligible for a refund."I spent a total of 480 bucks on this thing, and for someone at the age of 19, I simply cannot afford this waste of money.And even if this so called "plug" isn't actually included in the product, then at least help me find the missing piece I need.I might not be experienced in cars and I could also be wrong about what I said in the beginning, it might just be called something else or it might just be an entirely other piece that I'm talking about. My main point is that there is literally no way for me to reach out to AUTODOC because of their awfully designed AI assistant.
supplier_response: Hello ! We’re very sorry to read about your experience and understand how frustrating this situation must be, especially given the importance and cost of the part. Regarding your case, we would like to clarify that we have attempted to contact you, however the phone number and email address provided with the order appear to be invalid, which prevents our team from reaching you directly. To properly investigate the missing metal plug, check the product specifications, and offer you the correct solution (replacement part, clarification, or return options), we kindly ask you to contact us directly by email at trust@autodoc.de. Please include clear photos of the received steering rack, and the area where the plug should be installed. Once we receive your email, our support team will review the case manually and assist you as quickly as possible. We truly regret the inconvenience and appreciate your patience. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: 2 out of t: I ordered a set of headlights for my Polo in May after someone smashed them backing into me. I had them fitted with my annual service in October (I am self-employed and didn’t wanna lose an extra day of work and income). Upon fitting the garage realises one of the lights is broken so I issue a warranty claim. They send me a replacement part very quickly- so far, so good.However, the part I was sent is for brakes. So, I send it back using their return label. It arrives on the 24th of Nov (I have proof of tracking). And I wait. But no light. All I get is emails that details are missing. At first it’s my address. Then they say the returned parts haven’t been received. Last week their customer service assures me the case will be escalated and prioritised. Today they email me that they are closing it. More assurances that the warranty department will take care of it. Nobody can tell me what details are missing. There is no contact for the warranty department or an email address to contact them. I’m at a point where I just want a refund for the headlight I paid for.Summary: If nothing goes wrong they are a decent provider of parts. As soon as something goes wrong, it’s shambles and unnerving and either ineptitude or stalling tactics. At the moment, would absolutely not recommend.Update 19/12/2025: Going up from 2 Stars to 3.5After posting my initial review they contacted me and settled he issue swiftly. All resolved but it's still not impressive that it needs a Trustpilot review to get proper help. Will use them again but cautiously. They have to win back full trust. Once that's achieved I'll happily review again.

review_text: After posting this complaint, I was eventually contacted and reimbursed for the incompatible parts. However, after a few messages, the employee who had initially reached out to me appeared to stop responding, which forced me to contact the in-app support again. Only then was a warranty claim filed and my money refunded. While I am grateful for the resolution, I remain concerned about the way it was handled.The company referred to this outcome as a ‘gesture of goodwill.’ In reality, complying with consumer protection law is not goodwill—it is a legal obligation. What troubles me most is that, instead of straightforward assistance, I personally had to spend a great deal of time researching laws, studying specific legal articles, and presenting them to prove that I had the right to a refund.I appreciate the final resolution, but the process was unnecessarily time-consuming, frustrating, and placed the entire burden on me as the customer.Order number: 119912765Order date: 10.09.2024I am extremely disappointed with Autodoc’s customer service and their refusal to take responsibility for an incorrect part selection.All my previous orders for my Honda Accord 8 (VIN: JHMCU35809C203590) were always placed through Autodoc’s support team, never by myself. I always relied on their staff to provide the correct parts. However, in this case Autodoc now falsely claims that I ordered the parts on my own without their involvement, which is absolutely not true.The lower control arms that I received do not fit my vehicle. This is a clear case of “Sachmangel” under §434 BGB (German Civil Code) — the goods do not conform to the contract, as they are not suitable for the intended purpose. According to §439 BGB, in such cases the seller is obliged to provide a correct replacement or a full refund, and also to compensate the customer for costs of removal/installation.Instead, Autodoc is trying to hide behind a 14-day return period, which is irrelevant here. The 14-day return policy only applies to voluntary returns. When the goods are not as described or do not match the contract due to the seller’s mistake, the time limit does not apply — this is confirmed by EU consumer law (Directive 1999/44/EC and Directive (EU) 2019/771 on consumer sales and guarantees).In other words:•	The parts were selected incorrectly by Autodoc staff (as in all my previous orders).•	The parts do not fit — this is legally a non-conforming good.•	Autodoc must provide a replacement or refund under BGB §§434–439 and EU Directive 2019/771.•	Refusing support and shifting blame onto the customer is a breach of EU consumer rights.I am now forced to escalate this case to the European Consumer Centre (ECC-Net) and file a formal complaint. This whole experience has shown me that Autodoc’s customer support is unwilling to take responsibility when their own mistake causes problems for the buyer.⚠️ Buyers beware: if Autodoc makes a mistake in selecting your parts, they will try to deny responsibility and hide behind irrelevant “return policies.”

review_text: I ordered some parts from AUTODOC and when they arrived in the UK at the EVRI parcel delivery depo, the EVRI tracking system told me “This parcel is bigger than we expected, so a specialist Evri courier will need to deliver it.”So it is coming up to 5 days that the parcel has been with EVRI, and I am still waiting to hear when my parcel is going to be delivered.I contacted AUTODOC told them the parcel was oversized.   AUTODOC said “It is very unlikely that the package was oversized. All parts are inside normal package 750x200x950”.I have confirmed with EVRI and their size limit is “Add together the 2 shortest sides and multiply it by 2. Then add the length. If the total is less than 245cm, your good to go”.So doing this with the dimensions AUTODOC gave me for my parcel (750x200x950) the calculation would be 200 +  750 = 950.   Then 950 x 2 = 1900.Then take the 1900 + 950 = 2850!   A total of 2850mm shows this parcel is over the EVRI size limit of 2450mm.So it is oversized for EVRI based on AUTODOC’s own admissions.EVRI also tells parcel senders “If your parcel is Overweight or Oversized then we’ll send the additional costs on to the sender and keep the parcel until we receive payment”.So if AUTODOC don’t admit what they have done and look into what I have told them (will AUTODOC do this, because as far as AUTODOC are concerned the delivery is still not classified as late or there isn’t a size issue as far as they are concerned) so looking at other complaints on Trustpilot this could be weeks before this is sorted out.I have to also add this is the Second order in a row now, I have had a delivery issue with.So I have 4 direct questions for AUTODOC which are as follows.1)	Why do you send out oversized parcels to EVRI?2)	Even if this was an AUTODOC accident sending it via EVRI, why does you customer service team not know about the size limit with EVRI and go immediately to resolve the problem?3)	Is the issue I have having also happening to others?4)	When am I going to get my parcel?AUTODOC if you reply to this post, please give actual direct answers to all my questions and not some standard holding comment like “Your case has been referred to the quality control department and will be considered as soon as possible.”Thank you.John
supplier_response: Hello John, We are sorry you faced inconveniences. We will check situation with the parcels' sizes. Our quality control manager will contact you in the nearest time. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Few months ago I purchased a product on eBay from ATP Autotelie, now it broke because of a clear defect (it broke in two parts), and due to its breakage I was close to get my car engine completely burned. I had to spend more money to replace the defective product on an emergency basis, with a lot of time waste.Not even three months were passed, so the product was and still is under purchase warranty coverage, which is defined by the UE Council directive 85/374/CEE of 25th July 1985.However, ATP Autotelie has blocked all ways to contact them or rise any kind of issue.Therefore, I tried try to assert my customer rights through the PayPal claiming system, but the seller invented the excuse that somehow I “altered” the product, which is clearly false and can be easily verified from the picture I sent them. Anyway, PayPal decided to support the fraud and rejected my claim.The matter does not end here, I am reporting what happened (all of which can be proven with documentation in hand) first here on TrustPilot, and then I am turning to a consumer defense association to take legal action, not only against the seller but also against PayPal.This is not so much for an economic problem, but because people cannot be defrauded in this way, it is a question of principle and a question of law, both national and European community laws.**** Update ****After the review was published on TrustPilot, I was contacted by ATP Autoteile, unlocking the possibility to message them on eBay messaging system (but why it was blocked, nobody said).They behaved like they didn't know anything about the claim, that looks quite suspicious to me: did eBay arbitrarily take the initiative to reject my complaint without even informing the seller and without any reasoning? Let's take this as the truth, since the level of eBay service is so poor that can actually arrive at this point.After this, they formally apologized (formally) and accepted to refund the faulty product (except shipping costs), without wanting the broken product back. Then. we had days and days of endless email exchange to undergo the steps of the refund process, several their emails were also sent me twice from different email address. If I could count all the time I lost to reply and send all the information they asked, probably I had to receive three-four times the due amount, but probably this was exactly the idea under such behavior.Obviously I just received the refund of the faulty product (except shipping costs), that is already a huge step on towards the right direction, but the experience was so frustrating that I cannot avoid helping others to avoid my same mistake.

review_text: Your typical shady online retailer. Good prices, fast delivery as long as there's no problem, ordered quite a few things but never more. That's when they show their true morale. I'll edit this when I have the time, but in short: They will go through some lengths to save pennies (wrongly) and don't mind breaking written agreements to do so, even when they mess up in several ways on the same deliviery (deliberately ignoring cruicial emails, loosing the package, sending the wrong shock absorber packed in beat up/ducktaped box for the right one, promising free deliviery for all the mess but backing from that promise in the end when they once again mess up). When they see it's a never ending story (completely their own fault), they decided it'll probably cost to much to honor the agreement (despite me spending hundreds of euro for a lot of parts in the same order)  and just back away leaving me stranded witouth the most cruicial part, one out of four shock absorbers.The "customer service" is a joke, they have no feeling for right and wrong and will never go outside the manuscript, regardless if they messed up completely and put a customer in a seriously bad situation. Even the people in charge of customer service totaly lacks basic knowledge or just don't care.Will be posting the email conversations when I have the time so everyone can read it and weep. More than 70 emails back and forth over the course of almost 3 months to deal with the whole shitstorm with a bad ending (yeah, that's right) so will pack it up nicely.Stay away, trust me. Truly, truly horrible organisation.EDITReply to ATP:s reply: Yes, you refunded me the for the "second" shipping cost because you should have added that item in the forst order, you lost my order, it was greatly delayedand you sent me the wrong item in an opened and beat up box.So I have paid ONE shipping cost already according to our agreement, but still not recieved the item you agreed to send free of shipping.When you sent me the wrong shockabsorber in a opened and beat up box, which I sent back to you, you only refunded the shockabsorber and you refuse to send me a new one without me paying for the shipping cost again!Maybe it's easier to understand if I put it like this:1. First order. Lost, delayed and wrongly handled. Shipping paid.2. Second order. The item that should have been added to the first one (plus I added a bunch of other things=more money for you). Shipping cost paid again, and for the item of course.3. ONE shipping cost refunded according to agreement. (1 of 2 shipping costs paid)4. Wrong item recieved and returned.5. Wrong item refunded only. (Back at square one, with me missing an item that should have been sent in the first order.)6. You refuse to send a new one for some absurd reason, even though you have sent me the wrong one. Only reply is to place a new order and pay for shipping AGAIN.Do you still not understand?!

review_text: ***UPDATE*** company refunded cost of parts following this review.  A bit of a hassle unfortunately and I still had to cover the cost of replacing failed part and shock absorber as a result, so still cost me! ***  Rear Coil Springs:  Buyer beware!!!!   Bought these parts as they had a 3 year warranty - Failed within 12 months, unable to contact the company for replacement parts through Ebay as not accepting contact through normal channels.  Had to purchase replacements from another source and pay mechanic to remove broken parts and also replace shock absorber as that had failed as a result of the broken coil spring! review_text: I ordered brake parts at the start of July 2018, and after contacting the company it was agreed that I could refuse the package and have it returned for a refund.They were returned and I heard no news. I called them around mid August since I had no reply to any of my emails. I was informed that they were having problems due to a system upgrade (similar story to a lot of people here), but they had received the brake parts back on the 24th July. I was told that for some reason my refund had not been processed. The lady said she would make a note for it to be processed.I called again on the 31st of August as I still had not been refunded. I was told that for some reason my refund had still had not been processed, and that they would put another note to the correct team.I called again on the 17th of Sept because I still hadn't received my refund! The lady said it should have taken around three days... but "there was a mistake and it would need to be done again", but that she didn't know when I would be refunded.This is getting beyond a joke. I've waited nearly 2 months and still not received a refund. Absolutely shocking.I am now considering contacting my bank to conduct a chargeback.In response to ATPs reply below, I contacted the company with my details, and was again ignored. I have since opened a dispute case with my bank, who have now forcibly retrieved my money. I encourage others having problems to do the same.
supplier_response: Hi Doug, thanks for your feedback. Please send your Customer-ID or the number of your order to hilfe@atp-autoteile.de. Best wishes, Walter / ATP Servicecenter

review_text: I’m pretty sure that this review makes no difference to ATP or my case, but hopefully people will think twice before buying anything from this company.I purchased a Sachs clutch kit from ATP, for my Audi A4. The payment and delivery went fine and quick, which is probably the only positive thing I have to say about this company.We had scheduled a repair at a local mechanic for a gearbox and clutch replacement, so we delivered the ATP clutch as per agreement to the mechanic. However, when the mechanic went to fit the new clutch to the car, he noticed that the clutch didn’t fit and was actually a LUK clutch (completely different brand to Sachs). The clutch was branded LUK on one side and Sachs on the other.As the car was taking up space on a lift and the mechanic couldn’t wait for a new clutch to be delivered, we had to buy the correct clutch locally. Unfortunately it didn’t come with a clutch release bearing, so we had to use the one supplied with the clutch kit from ATP.I called ATP and explained the situation (in German, as apparently only one or two ATP employees speaks English) and asked if it was possible for me to return the incorrect clutch without the bearing, to which they replied yes.I returned the clutch as agreed, and for a long period of time I heard nothing from them, so I decided to call them to see if they received the clutch. After a couple of calls, they informed me that they had in fact received the clutch, but that the bearing was missing and that they could not refund me as the set was not complete. I tried to explain the situation several times with no luck and no sympathy.ATP sells the exact clutch release bearing on their website and I informed them, that I would happily pay for the bearing that I had used from the kit, so they could replace it in the kit. But for some reason this is impossible to do for ATP. In theory I could just get the clutch back, buy the bearing, put it in the box and return a complete set.I have now spent several hours on the phone, trying to first of all get through to them on the phone, which is a nightmare. And when I finally manage to get through, nobody speaks English (fortunately I speak a little German). The employees that answer the phone cannot make any decisions and needs to speak to their supervisors and promises to call back, which they NEVER do, so you have to start all over every time you get through to them.To make things even better, now ATP wants me to pay them to return the wrong clutch to me.

review_text: "no risk 90 day money back guarantee". Yeah sure.Bought a Chinese made A/C compressor for my Mercedes from them. It leaked badly. Not because it was broken or something. The design was bad and impossible to  create a sealed system.Sent the unit back. They made every single excuse you could imagine- I hadn't given them a chance to fix the problem, so no warranty (I did  they answered no questions about making the product actually work).- I didn't include the original mechanic's invoice - (I did, right on top of the compressor)- They inspected the thing and claimed  there was no proof of the defect occuring before the installation (simply, the replacement part was of a bad design - didn't work before, didn't work after, despite trying many things to prevent having to pay to uninstall and then install a different one)- The invoice didn't show what they needed to see - no warranty (my Italian speaking mechanic hardly understands what I ask him in english, however, I made sure he did the job right - flushing, replacing the drying unit, etc.)In short, they kept the part (probably re-sold it) and kept the money also!Edited October 25, 2018: ATP tried hard to remove this review about a year ago. Fortunately, after trustpilot looked at the evidence, this review remained. Don't be fooled by the feeble bluff of an offer to resolve the issue more than two years after the fact. Do yourself a favour and listen to my and all others' experience in buying cheap, poorly made non-functional parts and purchase your car parts from a reputable source. Otherwise, we told you so. My mistake cost me 179 euros for the compressor plus nearly 300 euros extra for my mechanic's huge efforts to try to make the thing work and then remove it and replace it with a unit from Mercedes.
supplier_response: Hi Maurice, thanks for your comment. We're sorry to hear about your experience. Would you mind emailing hilfe@atp-autoteile.de with a full report on your experience and we'll look into this. Kind regards, Walter / ATP-Customerservice

review_text: After my last review, I was contacted by Andrew, their Quality Control Manager, who apologized and gave me a €25 voucher. I decided to give them one more chance, and the experience has been even worse.The "Bait-and-Switch" is Systemic: Immediately after my new order, I received the same automated call stating "stock errors" and demanding I pay more for "expensive alternatives." This is not "dynamic pricing"; it is a recurring trap that ignores the price confirmed at checkout.GLS Delivery Failures: The taillight replacement they finally sent has not been delivered to my house. Like clockwork, GLS made no attempt to call or come to my address and simply dumped the package at a collection point. I am now forced to travel to pick up a part I paid to have delivered.Packaging Negligence: I have had two separate shipments destroyed because undercoating tins burst inside the boxes, ruining the entire order.The €25 voucher is just a way to keep you in a broken loop. If you need parts to get a vehicle back on the road, go elsewhere. Auto-doc.pt offers apologies in emails but continues to fail in the real world.I had got into the habit of ordering online, but was pleasantly surprised to have obtained all the parts I needed - some rare - from my local parts store, in one afternoon!
supplier_response: Hello Luis Alves Pereira! We sincerely apologize for the repeated issues you’ve experienced, including pricing confusion, delivery failures, and packaging problems. This is far from the service we aim to provide, and we understand how frustrating and inconvenient this has been. Our Quality Control Manager will be contacting you shortly to review your case and assist you further. Best regards, AUTODOC Team

To work with them, and GLS, their couriers, is a task in itself where you're more likely to lose money and time than get on with your repairs.

review_text: Ordered part for vehicle. Site said it was suitable for vehicle. Part wrong size for vehicle. Contacted Autodoc and they said return it but at my cost. The cost of return would have been more than item, convenient for them to avoid any returns, shipping had been charged already to be supplied incorrect part. Wouldn't recommend this company as they make a mistake and you bear the cost.  Follow up from Autodoc to my complaint was to supply the correct part at no further cost but they say you have to supply original part number or vin number to confirm correct part selection. Seems that there system doesn't link vin to registration number which seems strange! As for supplying OEM numbers are not practical to do on some items as this means the vehicle would be unusable while waiting for parts, not really a reasonable thing to do for most people. I eventually received the correct part at no extra cost after customer care got involved. The customer care were very good and keen to keep me happy. I received a voucher as a good will gesture also. I still think they need to rethink their policy on   wrong parts supplied as they are the experts and when it says it fits your vehicle then it  doesn't, it should not cost you anything to return and to charge an extra premium on shipping terms to avoid this isn't acceptable. I have changed my rating to ok but will be careful of ordering anything that I need urgently. Again I was treated well by customer care.
supplier_response: Hello Bruce, We're incredibly sorry about your disappointment with our service. Please note that by registration number you can only limit the list of parts to parts that fit your car model. The final check for compatibility is possible only with the help of the OEN (Original Equipment Number). Before placing an order, you should check the OEN indicated in the description of the item. Also note, that we offer a SO (Safe Order) option at checkout which allows you to return within 200 days and gives you a free return label. Please contact us at help@autodoc.de and provide your order details, so we can check your case. Best Regards, AUTODOC Team

review_text: Poor dispatch times and customer support for this order.I have ordered with Autodoc many times before and in 3 different countries, the parts and packaging are very good and generally the order comes within 2 weeks. However on this order I have been very disappointed. The order was taken on the 29th of April and I checked all the parts were in stock before the order was made. Payment was taken out of my bank on Wednesday the 1st of May but it took until the evening of the 7th of May for the parts to be dispatched. I contacted Autodoc customer support today the 13th of May as I was concerned that the last entry on the Tracking from the courier was made at 7pm on the 7th of May. I asked the customer support agent if they could contact GLS on my behalf to confirm that they actually had the parcel, as when I contacted GLS this morning I was told that only the ticket had been produced and they did not have the parcel. All the customer support agent did was copy the link to the parcel tracking that said it was in Germany to me and said I would have to wait till tomorrow or Wednesday for the parcel tracking to be updated. I am now left to wait, hoping that the Parcel is going to come but not knowing whether GLS actually have it or not. When I contacted customer support I at least expected them to contact the delivery company to confirm that my Parcel was on route and not lost.EditThe Parcel arrived Today the 14th of May. It was as usual well Packaged, I just wish that when I contacted the customer service they could have checked the tracking for me so that I knew that the parcel wasn't lost in transit rather than just telling me to wait and see!

review_text: For the second time I have used this website it WILL be my last. Firstly the items show up as in stock but shipping wasn't made until 8 days later. Over three weeks later and I'm still waiting. Secondly the items I ordered were a recognised quality brand Magneti Marelli but after paying for the items I got an email with an alternate unknown brand called "spark". THIS IS NOT WHAT I PAID FOR! No quality, no stock, no prompt delivery, no reliability, no honesty, if you need to fix your car please go anywhere else, this is a complete waste of time.UPDATE: Parts now received.I have been emailed by AUTODOC twice now, offering an apology and the possibility to return my items in exchange for the original items I had ordered at their cost. They claim that this will take more time however to source the parts, again I say that on their website it shows as "in stock", it clearly is not. I was told that the parts were swapped for "STARK" parts because it would be quicker for me, adding that they would not do this to me in any future purchases. Having read several forums on this, it seems this is common practice with AUTODOC and they will exchange parts without notice. If it were a cheap like for like part then I would accept it no questions. This is not a sustainable business model, I hope AUTODOC will take this as constructive criticism and develop a form of integrity so as to respect and honour their customers choices and reasonable expectations. I am grateful that they offered to exchange the parts at their expense, however I cannot go any longer without my car.
supplier_response: Hi Leo! We are sorry for the inconvenience caused by your recent purchase. Please contact us at trust@auto-doc.pt. Our service Quality control manager will handle your case and will find a convenient solution for you. review_text: Upd.  10/18/2022Changed rating +1 star. Special thanks to Alex, who supported me from the German office (at his request, I am writing an update). Details belowOn the 3rd week, I unexpectedly received a letter from autodoc support that my order was delayed and I was given a coupon for 3 euros. The most interesting thing (I don't remember for sure ) is that this letter arrived just on the day when the goods were delivered to me.Despite this, I started a correspondence with support. The summary of the correspondence: thanks for the coupon, but I'm not going to order from you anymore, because your service is s h i t. At some point, an employee of the quality department from Germany joined in, who tried to smooth the situation.Through a series of correspondence with Alex (support from German), the value of the coupon was increased from 3 euros to 20 euros and with Alex's personal assistance in the following purchases. He also explained the reason for the delay. I, in turn, explained that I, as a client, I'm not interested in their problems. We agreed that the company will work on its mistakes.IMHO:- regarding the autodoc: let's see if I do order something. I will look at other competitors- my personal: no matter what country you are in, but if you don’t stand up for your rights and demand the best quality that you pay for, you will use s h i t t y service all the time we all should work on our improvementsOriginal commentPlaced an order and paid for it on Monday (September 05) after 14:00. Today (September 13) the status is "Order is awaiting receipt by the carrier". That is, 1 working week has passed from the moment of the order to the moment of its transfer to the postal service. I am silent about how long the delivery itself will take place.In fact, starting from the 3rd day, my order changed its status every day (it’s a pity that the client has no way to track this in the system). There was nothing unique in the order, at the time of ordering and payment all the goods were in stock, no one contacted me to say that there were difficulties and the formation of the order would take a whole week.At the same time, the website indicates that the order will be sent within 24-48 hours.Honestly, I do not understand how this is possible today for a large integrator when we all strive to speed up the delivery of goods/services to customers.  And I understand that I don’t have much choice but to go to some local store and buy what I need.I recently moved to Portugal, but in this service, I went back 10-15 years in the past. Perhaps this is normal for Portugal, but the company needs to improve this service

review_text: 🇫🇷 FrançaisLors de ma première expérience, j’ai rencontré certains problèmes. Cependant, pour renforcer la confiance et éviter ce type de désagrément, il serait très utile d’intégrer un système permettant d’enregistrer le numéro de châssis des véhicules.En effet, le fait de se baser uniquement sur la marque, le modèle et l’année ne semble pas suffisant. L’ajout d’une recherche par numéro de châssis, ainsi que la possibilité d’associer ce numéro à un véhicule enregistré, permettrait de réduire considérablement les erreurs de compatibilité, même si une compatibilité à 100 % ne peut jamais être totalement garantie.Si une telle fonctionnalité est mise en place, je serais tout à fait disposé à continuer mes achats sur votre plateforme.J’espère sincèrement que ce type de problème ne se reproduira pas à l’avenir. Merci à AUTODOC pour la résolution de mon problème.NOTE: De plus, j'accède à votre site web via autodoc.be, et la langue par défaut devrait être le français, mais il s'ouvre constamment en néerlandais, ce qui est très agaçant :)---------------🇬🇧 EnglishDuring my first experience, I encountered some issues. I contacted my bank, they said the merchant needed to cancel the money transfer. KFZ refused to do this. Obviously I will get the money back, eventually, but I will have to wait 22 days. On the other occasions this has happened the Merchants have cancelled the money transfer within 3 days.I have asked for an explanation why the sale was refused and why the cancellation of the money request could not be done sooner. I am still waiting for a reply from KFZteile.  Not good enough.

review_text: German is not my mother tongue so I am writing in English.I have ordered twice at kfzteile24.de and twice they managed to make mistakes.The first time they sent a wrong part. It was labelled wrong in their stock, mistakes happen and I could return the part at no cost (international shipment!), so I won't blame them at that point. But then I got the very same, wrong part again! They didn't learn from their mistake... So I had to send back again (at their expense), and had to wait again for the replacement. Finally I got the correct part. But it took a lot of e-mails with their customer support and four weeks altogether...Last week I ordered again, a fairly large amount of parts. They sent me an e-mail that one part wasn't in stock, so they offered to send another brand (more expensive) at the same cost. Hooray for their service, I really appreciated it. But what they actually sent me, was an opened box with a used part! It got scratches and dirt on it! As it was still useable and had cost me only 10 Euro (normal price in local stores 25 Euro so still a bargain) I decided to use the part anyway and sent them an angry e-mail instead. Which they never answered...A friend of mine also had bad experience with kfzteile24, ordered a cilinder head gasket for an 8V cilinder head and got one for a 16V cilinder head instead. He had to talk endlessly to their customer support to get *their* fault rectified.I will hesistate to do business with them again. Pity, because if they didn't f*ck up every time, they would get five stars as they deliver quality branded parts for very reasonable prices.

supplier_response: Sehr geehrter Herr Schneider, vielen Dank für Ihre Mitteilung. 1. Eine Suche nur mit Fahrzeugauswahl oder anhand KBA-Nummer nicht immer ausreichend, da nur die FIN die Ausstattung sowie alle individuelle Besonderheiten einzelnes Autos berücksichtigt. Aufgrund OEM-Nummern kann man auch eine ausreichende Auswahl durchzuführen da diese Nummern zur direkten Übereinstimmung zwischen der gleichen Teile verschiedener Hersteller führen. Beachten Sie bitte auch was auf der Warenkarte steht: " Bitte beachten Sie, dass die abschließende Überprüfung der Kompatibilität nur mit Hilfe der sogenannten OEN ( Original Equipment Number) möglich ist. Die Beschreibung unserer Produkte enthalten nur technische Spezifikationen und können deren Kompatibilität mit einem bestimmten Fahrzeug nicht garantieren. Bevor Sie  eine Bestellung aufgeben , sollen Sie die in der Beschreibung des entsprechenden Artikels angegebene OEN überprüfen". 2. Sie haben beim Auftrag keine Option Sicher Kaufen gewählt, daher trägt der Kunde Rückversandkosten. Wir haben Ihnen unsere Anfrage per E-Mail gesendet und warten auf Ihre Antwort. review_text: This review is for the company Autodoc or autoteiledirekt.de (they seem to operate under a bunch of different names, so I posted here and on Autodoc reviews).The short version: Absolutely terrible customer service, bordering on a scam. Order somewhere else!The very long one for those who care:I ordered a bunch of parts from them and to be fair the items are what I expected and do their jobs but 2 days after the items were supposed to be at my house (shipped inside Germany so no excuse there) I called them up and after pretty unfriendly interaction with their customer service guy I got promised that it would go out that day. So, if it says 2 days, expect a week.While I was talking to him I noticed the parts where now 30 Euros cheaper on a 200 Euro order and I asked him for a deal. I would have taken store credit, even a complimentary sponge or a packet of gummy bears or something that showed any interest in their customers, but he wasn't willing to budge. Fair enough there too, they don't have to.So, I told him I would just order the stuff again and return the first package and since they already delivered the parts too late for me I had all the time in the world (second package was late by the same time frame too). I even bought their safe order (sicher bestellen) feature on the first order, which states that I can return the uninstalled parts for a full refund for any reason within a 100 days or something like that. Since the boxes are just sitting here and haven’t even been opened it shouldn't have been a problem.A week after I filed for the return I haven't even gotten a mail saying that it is being processed other than their automated instant reply so I called them up again. I didn’t even get an answer the first 4 times I called even though it kept telling me constantly I was next in line. After 15 Minutes and 27 Seconds you get dropped from the line each time so you get to try again. I have a headset and plenty of time while i was doing other things so I just kept calling. Even tried calling them from 3 lines at once (I got bored) and you get the same message on all of them. That was around 10am on a regular business day. Never got through to anyone then so I tried again in the afternoon and after the second round of waiting I actually got someone who couldn't even say hello or good bye or anything and I tried my best to be extra friendly. I got some results in the form of six emails (one for each part I ordered) saying my return was accepted and an attached PDF Return Form for every part. Seemed like progress but it also said that my return mailers would come in separate emails which still haven't arrived 5 days later.I guess I will listening to some more annoying music and automated messages next week. At this point I fully expect them to either not answer at all or at best be extremely rude again. Chances of me getting the full refund, that their extra safe order service promises are slim to none and I bet they will give me the full runaround for that too.Calling it what it is at this point is a Scam.I will change this review accordingly if they manage to surprise me and not do any of that, after all terrible service and late deliveries aren't a scam if the parts are what you bought but at this point it sure looks like a Scam when it comes to their return policies and safe order options.Update (26.06.2018):I guess complaining loudly on the internet does help since their english speaking customer service called me up first thing monday and sent me the return mailer (just one though even though I got two boxes but since they already had all the heavy stuff in one box and the 2 super light things in a seperate one I figured they might as well go in a single box. Didn't want to argue over this but I took a bunch of pictures anyway). The english speaking customer service rep was very nice, which was a welcome change so I guess I'll be carefully optimistic and wait.Update (09.07.2018):Turns out they actually did send me three labels - in yet again three separate emails - just two ended up in spam. I guess since it took 40something emails (not even kidding) to get to this point. So I sent the three packages in the same arrangement they came here and apparently they received them on Jul. 2nd. Still waiting to hear anything further. Don't hold your breath on getting your money in any sort of timely fashion back. Mind you I started the Process over a month ago on June 3rd. I do realize the return proves and banks and all that take their sweet time too. I don't have a problem with that, but this is just ridiculous. How does a company with that terrible of a customer service stay in business these days? I've head better customer service from Indian tech support scammers than these guys. I’m curious what happens next. My guess is I do get some money back at some point or another but not even close to the amount I paid with all kinds of bogus charges and “service” fees as a justification. I guess we will see.
supplier_response: Dear customer, thank you very much for your request. It is very regrettable to hear that you have not been satisfied with the terms of delivery. We would ask you to contact customer service for a solution with the forwarding company, thank you in advance for your efforts! We have sent you the return Form. Please print the return mark on an A4 paper, separate the return label at the marked point and stick it on your return. When you have some further questions, please contact us: trust@autoteildirekt.de. With the best regards, Your Autoteiledirekt Team

review_text: Deutsche Übersetzung weiter unten._______________________________It took 8 days to answer my matter about a product that did not fit!I ordered a lock cylinder (with two keys) with the correct OE number and to fit my car (also according to VIN code/ chassis number and registration number, but in the box was a completely wrong model. The support blames me that did not look up properly before purchase and that I am responsible for shipping (which costs more than the product is worth). Has sent pictures of comparisons between the products, but the support does not care and does not look up with the manufacturer. I do not know which cars it fits and therefore can not sell it, so I'll scrape it.Order from Autoteiledirekt if you think you will not have any problems with the products, but otherwise avoid!……….................................................Update!I have finally received free shipping back for the lock cylinder, but I think Autoteiledirekt has taken a long time to realize the mistake that is mainly the manufacturer's.It should be added that the number on the product itself is correct._______________________________Es dauerte 8 Tage, um meine Frage zu einem Produkt zu beantworten, das nicht passte!Ich bestellte einen Schließzylinder (mit zwei Schlüsseln) mit der richtigen OE-Nummer und für mein Auto (auch nach FIN/ Fahrgestellnummer und Zulassungsnummer, aber in der Box war ein völlig falsches Modell. Die Unterstützung beschuldigt mich, dass ich vor dem Kauf nicht richtig nachgesehen habe und dass ich für den Versand verantwortlich bin (was mehr kostet als das Produkt wert ist). Hat Bilder von Vergleichen zwischen den Produkten gesendet, aber die Unterstützung ist egal und nicht beim Hersteller nachschlagen.Ich weiß nicht, welche Autos es passt und kann es daher nicht verkaufen, also werde ich es kratzen.Bestellen Sie beim Autoteiledirekt, wenn Sie der Meinung sind, dass Sie keine Probleme mit den Produkten haben, aber vermeiden Sie dies!……….................................................Aktualisierung!Ich habe endlich kostenlosen Versand für den Schließzylinder bekommen, aber ich denke, dass Autoteiledirekt lange gedauert hat, um den Fehler zu erkennen, der in erster Linie der Hersteller ist.Es sollte hinzugefügt werden, dass die Nummer des Produkts selbst korrekt ist.

Why?   Because both of the rear springs on the OEMs had broken so any measurement would not have been accurate.However, if I could post pictures on this review, you - the reader  - can judge for yourself WHETHER the rear ride height looks normal.  With the OEMs, there was no differential ... I believe ... and even if there was, it was not noticeable to the naked eye.Look, maybe the Aerosus replicas will "settle down"  as the company claims. review_text: El mes de noviembre 2022 he comprado a través de eBay un catalizador para un Mini Cooper S cual me ha llegado con defectos en las roscas de los sensores y las chapas de sujeción también,cosa que he reclamado y me han devuelto 20€ de un total de 201€ ... hoy mes de octubre 2023( 11 meses más tarde )tenía que pasar la inspección técnica del vehículo y me han puesto falta grave por los gases porque el catalizador ya no hace bien su función.. según la garantía que dan por el catalizador la tienda BANDEL AUTOMOTIVE me deberían de devolver la cantidad entera de la pieza más la mano de obra de montaje cosa que yo no lo he reclamado y me he puesto en contacto con ellos a través de eBay por donde he realizado la compra y les he enviando foto del resultado de gases de la ITV pero ellos me dicen que la pieza ya no tiene garantía....parece que muchas tiendas online de Alemania están siguiendo los pasos de China tanto en calidad como la forma de los tratos con los clientes .... MUCHO CUIDADO CON ESTA EMPRESA 👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎...yo seguiré dejando este mismo comentario en todas las páginas que aparecen en la web además les voy a reclamar a través de la OCU y por lo menos recuperar el dinero del catalizadorIn November 2022, I purchased a catalytic converter for a Mini Cooper S through eBay, which arrived with defects in the threads of the sensors and the fastening plates as well, which I complained about and they refunded me €20 of the total. of €201... today October 2023 (11 months later) I had to pass the technical inspection of the vehicle and they gave me a serious fault for the gases because the catalyst no longer does its job well... according to the guarantee they give For the catalyst, the BANDEL AUTOMOTIVE store should refund me the entire amount of the part plus the assembly labor, which I have not claimed and I have contacted them through eBay where I made the purchase and I have sent them a photo of the MOT gas result but they tell me that the part no longer has a warranty....it seems that many online stores in Germany are following in China's footsteps both in quality and in the way they deal with them. clients.... BE VERY CAREFUL WITH THIS COMPANY 👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎👎... I will continue leaving this same comment on all the pages that appear on the website and I will also complain to them through the OCU and at least recover the money from the catalyst

review_text: This is a disaster. I don't recommend this company to anyone. I ordered a rim, they sent me a different size, I sent them back at my own expense, they re-sent them to a different address, they returned them, and they re-sent them, and the rims arrived two months later. I opened the package and, to my surprise, the rim was scratched! It also had two paint chips. Of course, the seller doesn't care. What ignorance and treating customers like garbage. Never again. The matter has been referred to the Consumer Federation.This is a response to lies, lies, and more lies.. You're just trying to exonerate yourself, but the truth is, as I wrote, you don't care about customers or the regulations that protect them. The company's policy is focused on salespeople, not customers. So it's no wonder customers are dissatisfied with your company's service. You run a business based on commissions from other companies and thrive on ripping off customers, which you profit from, but you don't care about customer rights. and you are still lying that you sent the parcel to Poland when no one gave you such an address. This company should be closed. The financial and tax authorities should deal with you. You are selfish. How can someone send damaged goods and tell them you're not interested and the matter is closed? This is just rudeness, not a serious company.
supplier_response: Guten Tag, vielen Dank für Ihre Bewertung. Wir bedauern sehr, dass Sie den Vorgang so negativ wahrgenommen haben. Gerne möchten wir den Ablauf jedoch richtigstellen: Sie haben uns mitgeteilt, dass die Felge ursprünglich in einer falschen Größe bestellt wurde. Eine Rücksendung ist in diesem Fall selbstverständlich möglich, allerdings haben wir Sie vorab transparent über die anfallenden Rücksendekosten in Höhe von 14,88 € informiert. Die Felge ist bei uns anschließend bereits beschädigt und zerkratzt eingetroffen. Aus diesem Grund konnte die Rückgabe leider nicht akzeptiert werden, da eine Rücknahme nur bei unbeschädigter Ware möglich ist. Die Felge wurde daraufhin, wie von Ihnen gewünscht, an Ihre Lieferadresse in Polen versendet. Wir bedauern, dass es zu Unannehmlichkeiten gekommen ist, möchten jedoch betonen, dass wir den Vorgang entsprechend unserer Rückgabebedingungen und nachvollziehbar bearbeitet haben. Herzliche Grüße aus St.Johann Ihr Team von rsu_gmbh

review_text: Hello all, I just need to leave my feedback for an ordered placed by Monday 21/07/2025. I order it and Thursday it comes the courier to leave the package as UPS informed but there was nobody at home and also the courier didn’t left any note or advice that he passed by. After figuring out via track and trace they mentioned on the tracker the package will be delivered next day by Friday. Waiting for the package next they Friday, UPS uploaded the status of my package as damaged. Straight away I have called to customer service from NL and a lady names Kasia she attended me saying that she received an email from UPS with information the package is damaged. I told her to send me another package with all same items if those ones are damaged. She told me that the rules are different ones and that I have to wait until the package will arrive back to the warehouse for evaluation and then I will receive my money back, in the meantime I can order again paying of course for the second time. I explained her in the phone that is not fear that I should solve this situation spending money from my pocket for some items I didn’t even receive and she told me that these are the rules and she cannot do nothing about it. Now my question is, why the customer service didn’t want to cancel my order knowing there is a problem with a damage package, also explained her on the phone that the car is dismantled on the garage and every day I have to spend money for keeping the car there because it’s about water pump and the dual mass from the gear box. The only response I have received it was that Che cannot talk to me anymore because I do not want to understand the rules she explained me on the phone and above mentioned on this complaint. I hope somebody from Motointegrator that supervise the orders can give an answer to this because when I asked lady Kasia for a supervisor or somebody who’s has power to decide she said that there is nobody who can help me. In my opinion this lady shouldn’t work anymore on customer service.
supplier_response: Dear customer, Thank you very much for your detailed explanation. We are truly sorry to hear that your experience with our delivery and customer service has been so disappointing. We fully understand how frustrating this situation must be for you, especially given the urgent need for the parts and the costs you are incurring as a result. Unfortunately, in the event of damage during transport, we are dependent on the return procedure of the carrier, in this case UPS. According to the applicable procedures, we can only process a refund or a new shipment after the damaged package has been returned and received by us. However, we understand that this procedure is not ideal in situations such as yours, where time and clarity are crucial. We regret that you felt you were not heard in your conversation with our colleague, but in her defence, I can only say that she and the entire customer service team are highly competent. She gave you the best solution within her capabilities. I would like to add that the company is now so large that it is no longer possible to quickly complete a new order from the warehouse. The warehouse is fully automated. Processes that have been set in motion must run their course as they are. Perhaps this is not so much about the rules we have to follow, but rather the procedures that cannot be interrupted in such a large company. Customer service works online from home. And my colleagues in customer service are very competent and friendly. I hope you can understand this. We would still like to help you as best we can. If you have any further questions or requests, please contact us again so that we can see if we can still arrange something for you within the existing possibilities. Kind regards, Susanne

review_text: I’ve ordered several times from Autodoc. Most of the time, the parts were correct, but they were often poorly packed and exposed to potential scratches or damage.My last order (No. 150947754), however, was a very disappointing experience due to customer support and company policies. The order included, among others, two similar items — one of which arrived in an open package and missing components.I filed a complaint, and the first solution offered was a compensation for the incomplete part in exchange of me accepting the part, which I refused because I needed the full product. Autodoc then informed me that I would have to return the part through a postal office and wait up to six weeks for a replacement.This is unacceptable — I had already paid in advance, received an incomplete product, and now would need to wait another 3–6 weeks for a replacement. Awful customer service and unreasonable return/replacement policies.I have submitted an official complaint and will escalate the case to the European Consumer Centre and Consumer Protection authorities as it looks to me as an abusive approach from Autodoc related to replacement of uncompliant products. Their mistake, but they take no responsibility and no risk to solve the situation. It is simply not acceptable to wait a total of 4–7 weeks from the initial order to receive the correct items.It is the customer that takes all the risk and headaches in a commercial relationship with Autodoc. They charge you in advance,  there is a high level of luck implied to receive all parts complete and undamaged, if there is a problem and you need something replaced you need to take the item to la postal office, wait until received and inspected by Autodoc, then wait a new delivery, hopefully with the right and compliant product… they take absolutely no accountability in this process it is only customer’s money and nerves…I advise avoiding Autodoc and choosing alternative suppliers with more customer-oriented policies and responsive support.
supplier_response: Dear Vlad V., We apologize for the inconvenience caused. There are standard return processing times. We are always committed to handling each case as quickly and efficiently as possible. Your case has been referred to the quality control department and will be considered as soon as possible. If you have any question, feel free to contact us on trust@autodoc.eu. Best regards, Your AUTODOC Team

review_text: Terrible, frustrating and disgusting customer service. WARNING do not purchase unless you use credit card or paypal type services for protection.They operate a return, refund and new purchase system which is slow, clumsy and open to abuse as in my case they proved their word is not to be trusted.I was delivered a 20mm thick core radiator instead of 40mm. I was treated as guilty from the first response and had to argue my innocence over the next two days. They implied I must have ordered the wrong part for my vehicle and asked to verify VIN etc which was all correct.They could not see the basic logic that it does not matter what car its for etc I ordered 40mm and got 20mm ....so wrong part.Eventually they wanted me to post back at my cost, more arguing they paid but I had to drop at a drop centre an hour away.I was promised refund 24hrs after they received it ( later other promises of 3-5 days and also if I close the dispute), 21 days of sheer frustration I finally got e refund via paypal dispute then escalated to a claim.Even then they left it till the last two days before Paypal automatic settlement, also a number of days of empty promises of settlement implying the delay is waiting for paypal.They never admit fault, in my case they blamed the supplier. The managers are spineless and lazy as they never call when asked.I had a 140km round trip to drop the wrong part off as I thought it would resolve things quickly..Wrong. (I live in rural location)I only had one apology from the one person who actually called me but he was only calling to review and clarify my position but I have received no company apology or compensation for the aggravation or the journey.If it was not for paypal I would have been really screwed!BUYER BEWARE if wrong or faulty parts deliveredThe latest reply, again blaming the supplier shows they take no responsibility...It does not matter if the wrong part was shipped directly by the supplier.Or if the chassis number etc is correct or no.The issue is the wrong size was shipped, photo evidence was provided that the dimensions where wrong.Instead of acknowledging a supply error and correcting it quickly at thier expense, they accused me of the error and made me fight for a refund... Now two years on they are still trying to get rid of my review... why?Even trust pilot reviewed my case after autodoc complained and tried to get it taken down...Paypal was about to refund me as their review was in my favour.My review is a true account of the facts. And despite their cheaper prices I have never used them again and warned others...

supplier_response: Hello Nour, Thank you for your review! We are sorry to hear that you had a bad experience at AUTODOC. We have checked your order and could see that you have ordered a spare part marked ''core part''. When you purchase such spare parts you have to pay a core charge in addition to the price of the spare part itself. According to European legislation, the core charge is the amount that the manufacturer requires as a deposit to guarantee the old spare part is returned for remanufacturing. In addition, this plastic cover (part) is loose and moves up and down when touched - this means that the plastic cover does not sit properly anymore.I handed over my car to replace the windshield since I read good review about car glass but not to damage the inner plastic cover of my premium car. I have attached the picture in the other email I sent to (socialmedia at carglass dot de) with the number plate and my contact details.Since the crack occurred when changing the windshield, it is requested to replace the plastic cover immediately and also to fasten it correctly so that it no longer moves up and down on touching. I am disappointed.Please respond to me asap how you are going to tackle this issue so I can follow up on it and respond to you legally.Update from 21Aug 2024: Herr Wahl from Carglass Unterhaching installed a new part free of cost. Also the part was ordered right away on my next visit for inspection and was changed with the appointment in a very convenient manner. Therefore, I am changing my review to Good back again.Thanks Herr Wahl for your help and updates.

review_text: Completely unserious, unprofessional and extremely rude treatment of a customer. I feel like I’ve been scammed. In short, what happened: I had previously ordered tires from this company and everything went smoothly. Now, suddenly, the delivery address was supposedly “incorrect,” and they demanded an additional charge of 14 euros per package (two packages). After I complained, they reduced the surcharge to 7.50 euros per package and stated that the first delivery had only been successful because I was “lucky.”In the end, after my complaint, they decided to return the packages to their warehouse and refund my money — but 30 euros less than I had paid. So not 7.50 euros per package, but twice as much. So, I won’t say anything more — you decide for yourselves whether I am to blame for an allegedly “incorrect” address to which all other packages are delivered without any problem, and whether a customer should be financially penalized for their mistake. I would also like to emphasize that they first ASKED for an additional payment, and when I refused, they charged me anyway.Let all potential future customers be aware of how buyers of this company's products are treated. I am certain that I will not spend another cent here. PATHETIC!
supplier_response: Dear customer, thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re sorry to hear about your recent experience and understand your frustration regarding the additional charges and the refund amount. We would like to review your case further to better understand what occurred. If you haven’t already done so, please open a ticket with our service after-sales team so we can investigate and resolve this matter as quickly as possible. If you already have an open ticket, know that your issue is being prioritized internally. We value your feedback as it helps us improve our service.

review_text: When my tyres weren’t delivered on the delivery date I was informed the next day that they were delayed with no expected date given.I decided to cancel my order, expecting an immediate refund. When my refund did not appear I called 123pneus, the lady on the phone was not particularly helpful and informed me that I wouldn’t get my refund until the tyres were back at the German depot. I explained that the contract with the carrier should be between 123pneus and GLS so why could they not cancel the order and refund my money?10 days after my original order, still having not received my refund and having ordered different tyres at a local garage, the tyres from 123pneus arrived at my home, despite cancelling the order one week earlier. I refused the package and they were returned to 123pneus.I eventually received my refund today, one month exactly after making my order. Very unsatisfactory cancellation policy when it’s not the customers fault. Despite having used them in the past I certainly will not be using them again. When things go wrong they don’t want to know. Do yourself a favour, buy local!!

However, things have gone downhill significantly in recent years.Two years ago, I received tires that were already a couple of years old—very disappointing. This year was even worse. I placed an order on 21.10.2024 (Order Number: 53653188) and waited for weeks, but the tires never arrived. I had to contact them multiple times via email, phone, and even WhatsApp before finally getting a response. Frustrated by the lack of communication, I canceled my order on 08.11.2024.I was told the refund process would take around three business days, but here I am, almost three weeks later, still waiting for my money. Despite contacting them repeatedly, I’ve received no updates or resolution.The service at Reifendirekt has become utterly unacceptable. What was once a reliable company is now a nightmare to deal with. I will never order from them again and strongly advise everyone to stay far away.

Wie bereits informiert, wurde Ihre Bestellung storniert. Es tut uns leid, dass Ihnen diese Unannehmlichkeiten entstanden sind. Wir hoffen, dass wir in Zukunft wieder die Möglichkeit haben werden, mit Ihnen zusammenzuarbeiten und Ihnen einen verbesserten Service bieten zu können. Wir setzen alles daran, unsere Dienstleistungen kontinuierlich zu optimieren, um Ihnen eine noch zufriedenstellendere Erfahrung zu ermöglichen. Vielen Dank für Ihre Geduld und Ihr Verständnis. Mit freundlichen Grüßen, Reifendirekt Team

review_text: This company is an absolute disaster. I place two orders for full set of wheels, incl. rims and winter tires. In one of the orders, the size of one rim was wrong - it took 4 weeks and 15 back and forth attempts to get in touch with them to return the wheels and another two weeks to get the money back. In the other order, the rim covers were missing and they are still missing 6 weeks later. Whatever money you save, you will regret that you are still dealing with them the moment something goes wrong with your order.Adding order numbers as requested by the company:53537916 - that's the order where one rim was of wrong size - it cost me a lot of trouble because I had to pick up the wheels from the garage, pack the wheels - they threatened that if they are not secured properly, I won't get my money back. Once the wheels got collected, it took almost 2 weeks to get the money!!! I actually had to pay the money to the garage because they already installed 2 wheels and the 3rd one was not correct so that they had to bring back my summer tires.53537914 - that's the order where the rim caps are missing - it's more than 4 weeks now that I'm not getting it sent. It's really unbelievable.1 star seems a lot in this case as 2 out of 2 orders got messed up and they cannot resolve them for 6 weeks.
supplier_response: Sehr geehrter Herr A. W., vielen Dank für Ihre Rückmeldung. Wir bedauern zutiefst, dass Sie mit unserem Service so unzufrieden sind, und möchten uns aufrichtig für die entstandenen Unannehmlichkeiten entschuldigen. Ihre Erfahrung entspricht keinesfalls unserem Standard, und wir verstehen vollkommen, wie frustrierend es für Sie sein muss, wenn Bestellungen nicht korrekt bearbeitet werden und die Kommunikation nicht effizient ist. Zu Ihrem ersten Auftrag (53537916), bei dem ein falsches Rim geliefert wurde: Es tut uns leid, dass es zu einer derart langen und komplizierten Rücksendung kam und Sie zudem zusätzliche Kosten bei der Garage hatten. Das ist selbstverständlich nicht der Service, den Sie erwarten sollten. Wir werden die internen Abläufe überprüfen, um sicherzustellen, dass solche Rücksendungen künftig schneller und reibungsloser abgewickelt werden. Bezüglich Ihres zweiten Auftrags (53537914) mit den fehlenden Felgenabdeckungen: Wir bedauern, dass auch hier die Lieferung nicht korrekt und in angemessener Zeit erfolgt ist. Das ist natürlich nicht akzeptabel, und wir werden sofort nachhaken, warum die Abdeckungen noch nicht versendet wurden, um dieses Problem schnellstmöglich zu lösen. Wir verstehen Ihren Ärger und bedauern, dass wir Ihre Erwartungen bisher nicht erfüllen konnten. Wir werden Ihr Feedback intern weiterleiten, um unsere Prozesse zu verbessern und sicherzustellen, dass solche Fehler in Zukunft vermieden werden. Es tut uns leid, dass Sie diese negativen Erfahrungen gemacht haben. Wenn Sie weitere Unterstützung benötigen oder noch Fragen zu Ihren Bestellungen haben, stehen wir Ihnen gerne zur Verfügung, um diese schnellstmöglich zu klären. The bit about reversing the bolt on my frame and collecting the adapter bolt could have been explained better, advising the torque setting. Initially I couldn’t lower the bike on the stand when locked up, but it’s easy pull the lever down and the lock releases and you can pull the lock out then lower.I have a tight space when fitting my bike and it takes about 10 to 20 turns to get my bike facing 180 degrees. review_text: Unfortunately, I cannot recommend dackonline.se based on my experience.I ordered a complete set of winter wheels on November 9th, with a promised delivery time of a maximum of 14 working days. This deadline passed by several weeks without me receiving any clear explanation. During this period, I was given conflicting information every time I called customer service – sometimes they claimed the tires couldn’t be delivered and tried to get me to cancel the order, while other times they promised that the wheels would be shipped “this week or next week.” None of it was true.At the same time, the exact same wheel package continued to be shown as “available” on their website, which felt both misleading and unprofessional.The customer service by phone cannot make any decisions at all; they only act as intermediaries and constantly refer to “the other department” that responds by email. This creates a Catch-22 situation – the phone support can’t help, and the email support doesn’t reply in time.Now they have also informed me that the product I ordered is sold out, despite it being listed as available during my entire waiting period. I have been forced to try to solve the issue myself, contact the Consumer Agency, and argue for an alternative, even though the fault lies entirely with them.It is extremely frustrating as a customer to have to chase answers, be transferred around, and receive different information with every contact. Especially when winter tires are legally required in Sweden and you depend on receiving your delivery on time.I recommend buying from a more reliable supplier that can actually keep their delivery times and provide consistent information to their customers.

review_text: I chose daekonline for new winter tires / alloys due to their excellent Trustpilot reputation (despite the items being cheaper on competing sites).As I had bought a new car on summer tires (and as it is winter time) time was of the essence. I therefore chose alloys and tires that daekonline.dk reported "in stock".After ordering I was immediately informed by mail that the order for the alloy had been sent to processing. Whereas I did not hear anything in regard to the tires (the alloys were no good to me alone – I need both alloys + tires and do not appreciate the order being split up in the first place).After 3 days I contacted daekonline.dk on telephone. I was told that, despite the tires still showing online as being “in stock” this was not the case. No guarantees could be made as to when these supposed in-stock tires would actually be shipped. On the other hand, I was assured that the alloys were in stock and were being processed for shipping.As I was in a hurry I cancelled the tire-part of the order and kept the alloy-part. I ordered the tires at a competing dealer that shipped the items immediately.6 days later – while now having received the tires from the competing dealer I had still not received a track and trace for the alloys. I made contact by telephone again.Now daekonline.dk could inform me that the alloys were not in stock either. Despite still showing in stock on their site, despite being reassured about the very fact days earlier on phone.Frustrated I cancelled the remaining alloy-part of the order, telling myself never to deal with daekonline.dk again.To make matters worse, after having received a confirmed cancellation on the alloys on mail I received a new mail from daekonline.dk days later again.Despite cancellation the item had been shipped. They now demanded that the shop that I had contracted to do work refused to receive the cancelled order that should never have been shipped in the first place.As of writing daekonline.dk has still not released the reserved amount on my account. Despite both order-lines being cancelled.I cannot stress strongly enough, never to deal with daekonline.dkJens Andersen on behalf of Ida Bojesen

Vi beklager ikke at kunne levere de bestilte produkter til tiden, og at du måtte annullere dine ordrer hos os. Der er allerede sendt en anmodning om at få reservationen slettet på din konto, kontakt venligst din bank. Vi håber du kan acceptere vores undskyldning for ulejligheden og at denne uheldige situation ikke vil forhindre dig i at gøre samarbejde med os igen i fremtiden, og at på denne måde, vil du give os mulighed for at forbedre din mening om vores virksomhed.
